items.title: Aquant Earns the Solutions Partner with Certified Software Designation for Manufacturing AI
items.description: This designation recognizes Aquant for delivering software solutions built on the Microsoft Cloud that demonstrate interoperability and meet program requirementsNEW YORK, March 04, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Aquant, a Microsoft partner and a leader in AI for asset-centric organizations, announced it has earned the Solutions Partner* with certified software** designation for Manufacturing AI within the Microsoft AI Cloud Partner Program. items.title: Microsoft Gaming Leadership Reset Puts Integration And Growth In Focus
items.description: Microsoft (NasdaqGS:MSFT) has appointed Asha Sharma as CEO of Microsoft Gaming, following the retirement of Phil Spencer after nearly 40 years at the company. The leadership change is part of a wider executive reshuffle and a refreshed direction for the gaming division. The transition comes as Microsoft continues integrating Bethesda and Activision Blizzard into its broader gaming ecosystem. For you as an investor watching Microsoft (NasdaqGS:MSFT), the gaming business is one of the...

items.title: Qualcomm Targets Robotics And AI Native 6G To Broaden Growth Story
items.description: Qualcomm announced a push into robotics with its new Dragonwing processor, built specifically for robotics platforms. The company also revealed a coalition with Amazon, Google, and Microsoft to develop AI native 6G, aiming for device demonstrations by 2028 and global rollout from 2029. These moves are intended to broaden Qualcomm’s business beyond smartphones into next generation connectivity and AI infrastructure. QUALCOMM (NasdaqGS:QCOM) is making this shift while its share price trades...

items.title: Big Tech signs Trump's AI energy cost pledge
items.description: President Trump announced a new "ratepayer protection pledge," saying energy demand tied to AI data centers could more than triple by 2035, promising American households won't see higher electricity bills as a result. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Jennifer Schonberger joins Yahoo Finance host Josh Lipton to discuss the big tech companies under the agreement, Google (GOOG), Microsoft (MSFT), Oracle (ORCL), OpenAI (OPAI.PVT), Amazon (AMZN), and xAI, and their pledge to fully fund new power generation and infrastructure needed for their AI facilities. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination.

items.title: Tech Bosses Tell Trump They’ll Pay Up for Electricity
items.description: Big Tech’s decision to foot more of the electricity bill has begun to benefit companies that provide power and their stocks.
